trade.summary: France and India have a growing bilateral trade relationship. France exports aircraft (Rafale jets), defence equipment, and luxury goods to India. India exports pharmaceuticals, textiles, and IT services to France. The 2023 strategic partnership summit deepened economic ties.

diplomatic.summary: France and India enjoy an 'exceptional' strategic partnership formalized in 1998. France was one of the first major powers to recognize India's nuclear status post-1998 tests. PM Modi's 2023 Bastille Day visit elevated ties. France supports India's UN Security Council permanent membership bid. Shared commitment to multipolarity and strategic autonomy.

military.summary: Deep defence partnership anchored by the Rafale fighter jet deals (36 jets delivered, additional naval variants ordered). France is India's second-largest arms supplier. Joint naval exercises (Varuna) held annually. Cooperation on submarine technology and nuclear-powered vessels discussed.

financial.summary: French companies are significant investors in India, particularly in energy (EDF, TotalEnergies), transport, and infrastructure. India's FDI into France is smaller but growing in IT and pharmaceuticals. Both countries committed to deeper investment under the 2023 strategic roadmap.

energy.summary: France and India have significant energy cooperation, particularly in civil nuclear power. EDF is involved in the Jaitapur nuclear plant project (the world's largest planned nuclear power plant). TotalEnergies has major investments in Indian renewables and LNG.

scientific.summary: Strong science and technology cooperation. Joint research in space (ISRO-CNES), defence technology, climate science, and AI. Many Indian students study in France. The Indo-French Centre for the Promotion of Advanced Research facilitates academic exchange.
